title: 怪钟疑案
summary:
Hercule Poirot instructs his companion to investigate a girl who may be connected to the case. Poirot suggests that because a specific phone call requested her presence at 19 Wilbraham Crescent, her involvement is not a coincidence. He advises the narrator to visit a blind woman, go to a printing shop under the pretext of printing a manuscript, and befriend the girls working there to gather information. Poirot concludes the conversation by quoting "The Walrus and the Carpenter" from *Alice's Adventures in Wonderland*.

During a trial in Crofton, Miss Weaver and Miss Martin provide testimony regarding a phone call received by the Cavendish Typewriting Agency. Miss Martin testifies that at 1:49 PM, a caller claiming to be Miss Pembroke requested a stenographer, specifically Miss Weaver, to go to 19 Wilbraham Crescent. Miss Pembroke denies making this call. Inspector Hatch testifies that he discovered the deceased's body at the residence immediately after receiving the call.

Dr. Riggs provides medical evidence, estimating the time of death between 1:30 PM and 2:30 PM. He concludes the victim was stabbed in the heart with a thin, sharp knife, possibly a French cooking knife, while in a coma induced by chloral hydrate. The victim had not eaten solid food for at least four hours prior to death. The coroner adjourns the trial for fourteen days, rescheduling it for September 28. Following the proceedings, Irena Brent of the Cavendish Typewriting Agency attempts to locate Sheila, who reportedly left the court with a young man. Irena approaches a policeman to ask for Detective Hakast, who is seen discussing matters with the coroner and the county police chief.
